2606.14199.title: OdysSim: Building Foundation Models for Human Behavior Simulation
2606.14199.paper_summary: Large language models are increasingly deployed as human simulators for interactive evaluation and social simulation. Yet helpfulness-driven post-training pulls them toward a homogeneous, overly agreeable assistant register, creating a behavioral Sim2Real gap. We present OdysSim, the largest open systematic investigation of behavioral foundation models, i.e., models trained to simulate human behavior at scale. We propose SOUL, a taxonomy of five capability axes (CONV, SS, COG, ROLE, EVAL) that unifies 62 datasets and 23 benchmark tasks under one framework. Specifically, we curate the OdysSim corpus (21.4M interactions, 10B tokens, retrofitted with back-generated social contexts), construct the SOUL-Index benchmark, and develop an end-to-end training recipe combining midtraining, task-specific RL, and expert distillation. The resulting open 8B OSim model ranks first or tied-first on 8 of 23 tasks, outperforming any individual frontier model by this count, with the strongest gains on conversational and social tasks. Its outputs are also more human-like in length, formatting, and word choice, and it transfers zero-shot to out-of-distribution user simulation on $τ$-bench, nearly matching real users on reaction alignment (93.2 vs. 93.5). We further show that LLM-as-judge RL induces reward-hacking patterns, and that our detectors can mitigate them during post-training. Together, our findings suggest that behavioral foundation models require rethinking the LLM training paradigm. We release all artifacts to support future research.
2606.14199.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型越來越多地被用作互動評估和社會模擬的人類模擬器。<lb/>然而，以有用性為驅動的後訓練使它們朝著同質化、過於迎合的助手風格發展，造成了行為上的Sim2Real差距。<lb/>我們提出了OdysSim，這是對行為基礎模型進行的最大規模的開放系統性研究，即在大規模上訓練以模擬人類行為的模型。<lb/>我們提出了SOUL，一個包含五個能力軸（CONV、SS、COG、ROLE、EVAL）的分類法，將62個數據集和23個基準任務統一在一個框架下。<lb/>具體而言，我們整理了OdysSim語料庫（2140萬次互動，100億個標記，並重新生成社會背景），構建了SOUL-Index基準，並開發了一個端到端的訓練配方，結合了中期訓練、任務特定的強化學習和專家蒸餾。<lb/>最終的開放8B OSim模型在23個任務中的8個任務上排名第一或並列第一，超越了任何單一的前沿模型，並在對話和社會任務上取得了最強的增長。<lb/>其輸出在長度、格式和用詞上也更接近人類，並且在$τ$-bench上進行零樣本轉移到分佈外的用戶模擬，幾乎在反應一致性上與真實用戶相匹配（93.2對93.5）。<lb/>我們進一步表明，作為評判者的LLM強化學習會引發獎勵黑客模式，而我們的檢測器可以在後訓練期間減輕這些模式。<lb/>總的來說，我們的發現表明，行為基礎模型需要重新思考LLM訓練範式。我們釋放所有工件以支持未來的研究。
2606.14199.author: Xuhui Zhou et.al.
2606.14199.authors: Xuhui Zhou, Weiwei Sun, Weihua Du, Jiarui Liu, Haojia Sun, Qianou Ma, Tongshuang Wu, Yiming Yang, Maarten Sap
